ranges.notes: Cold and wet weather; New Year events bring moderate crowds, but overall tourist activity is low.
Cold, damp, and often grey weather; very quiet period with few events or tourists.
Weather remains chilly and wet; some indoor cultural activities available but low tourist traffic.
Typically grey and cold with occasional rain; good for budget travelers seeking low crowds and cheap accommodation.
Cold and wet weather persists; some local indoor events but generally quiet for tourists.
Valentine's Day brings special events and dining options; weather still chilly but some romantic appeal for couples.
Weather remains cold but slightly improving; indoor cultural events and museums offer good options; crowd levels remain low.
Early March can still be chilly and wet, but days lengthen and some signs of spring emerge; moderate crowd levels.
Welsh national day festivities boost local culture and events; weather improves somewhat; good time for cultural travelers.
Late March sees milder weather and fewer crowds than spring peak; good for outdoor activities and exploring city parks.
Spring weather improves with mild temperatures; food festival attracts visitors; moderate crowds and lively atmosphere.
Pleasant spring weather; ideal for outdoor sightseeing in parks and Cardiff Bay; low to moderate crowd levels.
Easter attracts families and tourists; good weather usually; higher crowd levels and prices but many cultural and family events.
Mild and often sunny weather; outdoor events and festivals start; moderate to high crowds around holidays.
One of the best months for weather with warm, sunny days; ideal for outdoor activities and sightseeing; low to moderate crowds.
Late May remains mild and pleasant; some events start leading into summer; crowds growing but still manageable.
Early summer with warm weather and long daylight hours; excellent for outdoor sightseeing and events; moderate crowds.

ranges.event: Cardiff International Food & Drink Festival (mid June)
ranges.notes: Food festival boosts local culture and tourism; warm weather and vibrant atmosphere; good for all traveler types.
Warm and sunny weather continues; ideal for exploring Cardiff Bay and outdoor festivals; moderate to high crowds.

ranges.event: Cardiff International Food & Drink Festival continues (early July)
ranges.notes: Peak summer weather with warm temperatures; food festival and cultural events; high tourist crowds and lively atmosphere.
Warmest month with long days; ideal for outdoor activities, festivals, and city tours; very high crowd levels and accommodation prices.
Continued warm and dry weather; peak tourism season; busy but vibrant city life; book accommodations early.
Summer festival season continues with Cardiff Carnival; warm weather; very high crowds and lively atmosphere.
Warm but occasionally rainy; summer crowds remain high but start to slightly decline after mid-August; good for families and outdoor activities.
Late August sees crowd levels decrease slightly; weather still warm; good for sightseeing and events with fewer tourists.
Early autumn with mild weather and fewer crowds; ideal for budget travelers seeking good weather and lower prices.

ranges.event: Cardiff International Food & Drink Festival (late September)
ranges.notes: Food festival and cooler but pleasant weather; lower crowds than summer; good for cultural tourists and families.
Autumn weather cools and becomes wetter; fewer tourists; good for indoor cultural activities but outdoor sightseeing less pleasant.
Cooler temperatures and some rain; Halloween events attract moderate crowds; good for families and couples interested in seasonal festivities.
Cold, wet and often grey weather; low tourist numbers; good for budget travelers but limited outdoor activities.
Chilly and damp with short daylight hours; few events; low crowds and low prices but weather may deter some travelers.
Cold and wet but festive atmosphere begins; Christmas markets start attracting visitors; moderate crowds in weekends.

ranges.event: Cardiff Christmas Market and Winter Wonderland
ranges.notes: Festive season in full swing with markets, events, and decorations; cold weather but vibrant atmosphere; high crowds on weekends.
Major holidays with festive events and celebrations; cold and often wet; high crowds especially on Christmas Eve and New Year's Eve.
